items.details: Doctorate funded by the Economic and Social Research Council (ESRC) Supervisors: Professor Tatiana Kirsanova, Professor Charles Nolan and Professor Richard Dennis Thesis examiners: Doctor Kalin Nikolov and Professor Campbell Leith Thesis Title: “AssetPriceBubblesandMacroeconomicPolicies”

items.institution: University of Glasgow, United Kingdom. Graduated with Merit
items.details: Two-year programme in economics structured as a Doctoral training Master’s Dissertation: “HowtoModelCreditFrictions?”

items.institution: University of Caen, France. Graduated with Merit
items.details: Exchange programme: the programme was taught and assessed at Texas A&M University, USA The Master 1 is the French equivalent of the Master of Science Master’s Dissertation: “AComparativeAnalysisofFedandECBMonetaryPolicies”
The Licence is the French equivalent of the Bachelor’s degree

I am primarily interested in theoretical macroeconomics, with research focusing on business cycle fluc- tuations, macro-finance, asset price bubbles, financial crises, and the design of macroeconomic policy. ResearchFields: Macroeconomics, Macro-Finance, Monetary Economics, Financial Stability

My paper studies the implications of the dynamic ownership of risky asset price bubbles between banks and retail investors for financial stability and real activity. The analysis shows that the severity of bank- ing crises and recessions depends on both the exposure of individual banks to the bubble and the overall contamination of the banking sector. The paper also highlights that the early stages of bubble growth increase the financial vulnerability of the economy, as banks increasingly invest in the bubble during this period. While stricter banking supervision can reduce the effects of a bursting bubble, it may also weaken banks’ resilience to economic shocks.

This paper develops and estimates a DSGE model with stock market bubbles and nominal rigidities using Bayesian methods. Bubbles emerge through a positive feedback loop mechanism supported by self- fulfilling beliefs. Their movements, driven by a sentiment shock, significantly contribute to fluctuations in investment, output, and inflation.

This paper develops a DSGE model to study stock market dynamics. A key novelty of the framework is the introduction of firm exit and equity issuance by new entrants. AssetPriceBubblesandMonetaryPolicy: DeflatetheBubble? This paper studies monetary policy in a New Keynesian model with asset price bubbles. It shows that monetary policy targeting asset prices can partially deflate an asset price bubble and affects the way the bubble is financed.

items.details: Winner of the Warwick Award for Teaching Excellence (Social Sciences Faculty Award), in recognition of an outstanding contribution to teaching and student support. The Social Sciences Faculty Award recognises the achievements of Warwick’s outstanding educators who have enabled excellent learning, creating the conditions within which all students are supported and empowered to succeed and thrive.

items.role: Fellowship of the Higher Education Academy
items.details: Awarded the Fellowship of the Higher Education Academy Academic during the 2023-2024 academic year upon completion of the Professional Pathway for Experienced Staff (APP EXP). This program, recognised by the Higher Education Academy, assists experienced staff in aligning with the UK’s Pro- fessional Standards Framework 2023 and in demonstrating their involvement in teaching and learning support in higher education.

Nominated, shortlisted as finalist, and awarded a commendation ( 3rd prize) in the Warwick Award for Teaching Excellence (WATE Social Sciences Faculty Award), in recognition of outstanding contribu- tions to teaching and the support of student learning.

Nominated and shortlisted as a finalist in the Student Teaching Award for Best Graduate Teaching Assistant of the University of Glasgow. The award recognises teachers who have made an excellent contribution to the learning experience, inspired students to study beyond the classroom, and show clear passion and excitement for their subject matter.
Certificate of Excellence in Teaching for passing the Developing as a Teacher in Higher Education course
